title: Head of Revenue Operations
description: Finout is hiring a Head of Revenue Operations to build and scale the operational backbone of its GTM engine, reporting to the CRO. Requires 7+ years in Revenue Operations, Sales Ops, or Strategy in high-growth B2B SaaS, with proven experience scaling beyond $30M+ ARR. Needs deep Salesforce administration and revenue analytics expertise, strong cross-functional leadership across Sales, Marketing, Finance, and HR, and experience with globally distributed teams collaborating with the Tel Aviv HQ. A strategic, hands-on builder-doer mindset is essential.
score_reason: Exceptional match: 15+ years directly exceeds 7+ requirement, Fintech vertical is strong fit, Salesforce admin expertise aligns perfectly, proven ability to scale GTM operations in B2B SaaS, and Tel Aviv hybrid location matches preferred geography.
ai_opener: Omri, I noticed Finout is building out their Revenue Operations function from the CRO level—this feels like a natural fit given your track record scaling GTM operations in high-growth B2B SaaS and your fintech background. With your Salesforce expertise and experience orchestrating cross-functional alignment across Sales, Marketing, and Finance in distributed teams, you'd hit the ground running on day one. Worth a conversation to explore how you'd structure their operational backbone?

title: RevOps Manager, Territory Strategy & Operations
description: Gong seeks a RevOps Manager focused on Territory Strategy & Operations to accelerate revenue growth and seller productivity through data-driven investment strategy and scalable territory design. Requires 3–5+ years in Revenue Operations, Sales Strategy, or GTM planning, a deep understanding of B2B SaaS go-to-market models and segmentation, and strong analytical skills (Excel, SQL, BI tools like Tableau/Retool). Proficiency with Salesforce required; territory management tools like Fullcast, Pigment, or Anaplan a plus. Maintains territory mapping and leads market expansion and rep capacity modeling projects.
score_reason: Exceptional match—Omri's 15+ years B2B SaaS RevOps expertise, Salesforce proficiency, and deep GTM/pipeline forecasting background directly align with territory strategy, data-driven modeling, and seller productivity focus. Gong's AI-native platform and revenue acceleration mission fit his strong analytical profile perfectly.
ai_opener: Omri here—I've spent 15+ years architecting revenue operations and GTM strategies at scale in B2B SaaS, with deep expertise in territory modeling, pipeline forecasting, and rep capacity optimization. I noticed Gong is doubling down on Territory Strategy & Operations to drive seller productivity, and I'd love to explore how my background scaling revenue operations and building data-driven segmentation models could accelerate your growth trajectory.

title: RevOps Manager
description: Instrumentl, a venture-backed SaaS company building fundraising automation for nonprofits, seeks a RevOps Manager to own the technical infrastructure powering its revenue engine. This is a builder role architecting the CRM data model, integrating data sources, and leading AI tool implementation across sales processes. Requires 3–5+ years in Revenue, Sales, Marketing Operations, or GTM Systems at a B2B SaaS company, strong technical skills (SQL, complex workflows, APIs), hands-on CRM administration (HubSpot preferred), and data architecture/integration experience. Target salary band $120,000–$170,000 plus equity.
score_reason: Omri's 15+ years in B2B SaaS RevOps/GTM with deep HubSpot expertise, data architecture experience, and AI-native GTM background is a strong match for this builder role. The remote US position aligns with his location flexibility, and the technical CRM/integration demands align perfectly with his core competencies.
ai_opener: I've been building revenue infrastructure for B2B SaaS companies for 15+ years, and what Instrumentl is doing with fundraising automation caught my attention—solving the nonprofit tech gap is compelling. Your RevOps role reads like architecting a modern GTM data layer, which is exactly the kind of technical infrastructure challenge I've owned at scale: CRM data modeling, API integrations, and AI-first workflows. I'd love to explore how my experience could accelerate your revenue engine.

title: Head of AI & Revenue Operations
description: Noibu is hiring a Head of AI & Revenue Operations to own the full GTM software stack (Salesforce, Outreach/Nook) as admin of record and use AI tooling aggressively to ship automation. Requires 5+ years of revenue operations experience in B2B SaaS, with 2–3 years as a hands-on Salesforce administrator and Salesforce Admin certification. Includes leading renewal process automation, Clay enrichment workflows, ICP/TAM enrichment, and partnering with Sales, CS, Marketing, and Finance leaders. Demonstrated fluency with AI tools like Claude, Cursor, and Clay required.
score_reason: Exceptional match: Omri's 15+ years B2B SaaS RevOps experience, Salesforce expertise, and GTM stack ownership directly align with core requirements. The AI-native focus (Claude, Cursor, Clay) and automation-heavy mandate perfectly suit a senior executive seeking to leverage modern tooling at scale.
ai_opener: Noibu's mandate to aggressively ship automation through AI tooling and own the full GTM stack resonates deeply with my approach to RevOps—I've spent 15 years building scalable revenue systems, and the last few years architecting Salesforce automation and AI-driven workflows like Clay enrichment and predictive forecasting. Your focus on renewal process automation and ICP/TAM enrichment suggests you're moving beyond tactical admin work into strategic revenue architecture, which is exactly where I've been investing.

title: RevOps Systems Manager
description: Actabl, a hospitality-tech SaaS company, seeks a RevOps Systems Manager responsible for administration, optimization, and strategic oversight of Salesforce and other RevOps-owned systems. The role ensures seamless operation, scalability, and security of the CRM and integrated tools supporting revenue teams. Requires Salesforce Advanced Administrator and App Builder certifications, 5+ years of hands-on Salesforce administration managing complex orgs independently, SOQL proficiency, and experience integrating RevOps tools such as Outreach, Gong, PandaDoc, ZoomInfo, and LinkedIn Sales Navigator with Salesforce.
score_reason: Strong Salesforce admin expertise and RevOps systems focus align well with Omri's core competencies and seniority level. However, the role is more systems-administration focused than strategic GTM/growth-oriented, which may underutilize his senior executive experience.
ai_opener: Hi there – I noticed Actabl is building out deeper Salesforce governance and RevOps infrastructure. With 15+ years in B2B SaaS RevOps and hands-on Salesforce administration across complex multi-system environments, I've scaled CRM operations for hypergrowth teams while maintaining security and scalability. I'd be interested in discussing how to architect your RevOps tech stack to drive revenue velocity in hospitality-tech.

title: Revenue Operations Business Analyst
description: SmithRx, a venture-backed Health-Tech company disrupting the Pharmacy Benefit Management sector, seeks a Revenue Operations Business Analyst to serve as the primary liaison between RevOps and stakeholders. The role defines business needs, develops enablement materials, and owns adoption of processes, systems, and tools. Requires 3+ years as a Business Analyst or similar within a Revenue or Sales Operations organization, conducting deep-dive sessions with sales, finance, and legal teams, translating needs into requirements and user stories, and managing UAT plans. Jira experience and PBM/healthcare background preferred.
score_reason: Strong RevOps foundation and stakeholder management experience aligns with liaison/enablement focus, but role is positioned as Business Analyst rather than leadership level. Health-Tech/PBM sector is new territory, though B2B SaaS expertise and systems thinking transfer well.
ai_opener: Omri here—15+ years building RevOps infrastructure at scale across B2B SaaS. I've spent considerable time translating complex sales/finance/ops needs into actionable systems and processes, and I'm intrigued by SmithRx's approach to disrupting PBM. The cross-functional liaison role resonates—would love to explore how my experience scaling GTM operations could accelerate your revenue function as you grow.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Conveo, an AI research platform enabling fast, affordable B2B and consumer research used by brands like Unilever, Google, and Orange, seeks a GTM Engineer who can code, ship, and iterate on internal tools and automations that make the GTM team highly effective. As Conveo scales rapidly, the opportunity is to replace manual workflows and spreadsheets with purpose-built apps, pipelines, and AI-powered workflows. Work includes building lead scoring models that learn, attribution pipelines, faster customer onboarding flows, and AI agents that eliminate hours of manual work.
score_reason: Strong alignment with GTM Engineer title, AI-native tools, and pipeline/automation focus. However, the role requires hands-on coding ability, which is atypical for RevOps executives and may not be Omri's primary strength unless he has a technical background.
ai_opener: Omri, I saw you've built GTM engines at scale in B2B SaaS—Conveo is hiring a GTM Engineer to do exactly that, but with AI. They're replacing spreadsheets and manual workflows with purpose-built automations and lead scoring models that learn. Given your RevOps expertise in pipeline forecasting and CAC/LTV optimization, this could be a natural fit if you're looking to own GTM tech stack architecture.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Gradient Labs, a team of builders from companies like Monzo, Pleo, and Google, seeks a GTM Engineer for a foundational role in its GTM motion. You'll own the systems and signals behind identifying, segmenting, and reaching their ICP, stitching BDR, marketing, and sales ops into one streamlined operation, and partnering with AEs and the Head of Marketing to turn signals into pipeline. Requires 5+ years in rev ops, marketing ops, or sales ops at an enterprise SaaS company, a systems-building instinct, fluency in HubSpot/Clay/Apollo, and genuine AI fluency.
score_reason: Exceptional match—15+ years in B2B SaaS RevOps directly aligns with 5+ year requirement; GTM Engineer title is a target role; systems-building, HubSpot fluency, and AI-native GTM are core strengths. Remote location is ideal.
ai_opener: Gradient Labs' GTM motion caught my attention—the vision of stitching BDR, marketing, and sales ops into one unified signal-to-pipeline system resonates with 15 years of building exactly these systems at scale in B2B SaaS. I've spent the last several years architecting AI-native GTM engines and deeply understand the HubSpot/Clay/Apollo stack you're leveraging. I'd be interested in exploring how I could help you operationalize ICP segmentation and turn your signals into predictable revenue.

title: Lead Marketing & Growth Operations Analyst
description: Tebra seeks a Lead Marketing & Growth Operations Analyst to connect data to growth strategy and translate insights into operational levers and measurable revenue impact. Requires 5–7+ years in Marketing Analytics, Growth Analytics, Revenue Operations, or GTM Analytics within a SaaS or high-growth environment, deep understanding of marketing funnels, lifecycle stages, attribution models, and campaign performance, and strong SQL skills with modern data stacks (Snowflake, dbt, BI tools). Experience partnering with Marketing, Growth, and Sales stakeholders in fast-moving environments is essential; Snowflake Cortex AI or LLM tooling a plus.
score_reason: Strong alignment with Omri's RevOps/GTM expertise, data-driven mindset, and B2B SaaS background. The role's focus on connecting analytics to revenue operations and cross-functional stakeholder management (Marketing, Growth, Sales) directly matches his core competencies, though the Marketing Analytics emphasis is slightly narrower than his full RevOps scope.
ai_opener: Omri here—15+ years building revenue engines in B2B SaaS. I've spent my career doing exactly what Tebra needs: translating data into operational levers that move revenue. Your focus on connecting marketing analytics to growth strategy resonates deeply—I've built pipeline forecasting models, optimized funnel mechanics, and scaled GTM operations across teams. Happy to explore how modern data stacks and RevOps rigor could accelerate Tebra's growth.

title: Head of Marketing
description: Numeral, a venture-backed SaaS startup building the modern operating system for accounting teams, seeks its first GTM hire as Head of Marketing. Reporting directly to the CEO, you'll develop and execute the overall marketing strategy, owning branding, demand generation, sales collateral, thought leadership content, website design, press releases, and social media. The company is targeting a massive market (>$20B in the US alone). Ideal for someone wanting to get in on the ground floor of a B2B SaaS startup and scale the marketing function and team.
score_reason: Strong B2B SaaS pedigree and GTM expertise align well with the ground-floor opportunity, but this is primarily a pure marketing leadership role rather than RevOps/GTM Operations focus. The role emphasizes branding, content, and marketing strategy over the data infrastructure, pipeline forecasting, and operational efficiency that drive Omri's core value.
ai_opener: Omri, I came across Numeral's mission to modernize accounting operations—a space with massive GTM potential. Your 15+ years scaling B2B SaaS revenue engines would be invaluable as they build their first marketing function. That said, this Head of Marketing role leans heavily into content strategy and branding rather than RevOps infrastructure. Worth a conversation if you're pivoting toward marketing leadership, but wanted to flag that upfront.
